chunks.text: The EECS Honors Program is designed to provide very talented undergraduate students interested in undergraduate research. Honors students pursue an academic concentration outside of the department, engage in research, receive a special faculty advisor, and are invited to special events with faculty and EECS Honors alumni. Benefits include option to complete honors thesis and receive letter graded research units, official notation of honors degree on Berkeley transcript, faculty advising with chairs of the EE and CS divisions, annual event with alumni, and membership in Honor Program Linked-in group with program alumni.
chunks.question: What are the main benefits of the EECS Honors Program?
chunks.answer: Research opportunities, faculty advising, and alumni events.

chunks.text: Eligibility requirements: at least 45 units completed at UC Berkeley if admitted as a first-year student, or at least 12 units if admitted as a Junior Transfer. Minimum overall and technical GPA of 3.7. Must be declared in EECS, ECE, or Computer Science major. Must be part of the Honors program for at least one year, e.g., applying in Spring 2026 means graduating in Spring 2027 or later. Students must have lower-division major requirements completed or in progress, with specific courses for EECS, ECE, and CS majors. Junior transfer students may apply without CS 61C completed.
chunks.question: What are the GPA and unit requirements for EECS Honors eligibility?

chunks.text: Applications are accepted in Fall and Spring semesters only. Submit online EECS Honors Application Form including resume, proposed Program Planning Form, and an essay. Essay should address how breadth courses address a unifying theme and educational goals, with maximum length of 3000 characters. Optional petition for non-course research credit. Deadline for Fall 2026 admission is Wednesday, May 6th at 4 pm PT. Applications reviewed by committee evaluating essay, grades, course loads, breadth area commitment, and research interest.
chunks.question: What is required in the EECS Honors application essay?
chunks.answer: Address breadth courses and educational goals.

chunks.text: Requirements for completion: fulfill all Bachelor’s degree requirements for Computer Science, EECS, or ECE Degree. Maintain a 3.50 cumulative and technical GPA. Complete at least four units of “Supervised Independent Study” or other approved research credit. Study in a breadth area outside the EECS department, consisting of at least three upper-division courses totaling at least 10 units. Courses must be taken for a letter grade and cannot be cross-listed with CS/EE/EECS. Honor Students have option to complete an Honor Thesis by enrolling in four units of EE H196 or CS H196 during senior year.
chunks.question: What are the GPA and research requirements for completing the Honors Program?

chunks.text: Honors Program Class of 2025 includes graduates pursuing various paths such as PhDs at institutions like Stanford and Princeton, software engineering roles at companies like Meta and Apple, and master's programs at schools like Georgia Tech and ETH Zurich. Examples: Samuel Bobick pursuing a PhD, Circle Chen at Meta as Software Engineer, Ryan Cheng in EECS 5th Year Masters, Soohyuk Cho PhD at Princeton, Ryan Cottone PhD at Stanford, Anna Dawes pursuing a PhD, Alexander Di EECS PhD at Berkeley, Matthew Ding PhD at Stanford, Kevin Dong Business Analyst at McKinsey, Ria Doshi ML PhD at Stanford.
chunks.question: What are some post-graduation paths for Honors Program Class of 2025?
chunks.answer: PhDs, software engineering, and master's programs.

chunks.text: Honors Student Achievements include awards such as Arthur M. Hopkin Award, Goldwater Scholarship, Astronaut Scholar, Gates Cambridge Scholarship, and NSF GRFP Fellowship. Notable students: Yash Aggarwal (2024) Arthur M. Hopkin Award, Nitish Dashora (2023-24) Goldwater Scholarship and (2022-23) Astronaut Scholar, Simon Zirui Guo (2022) Arthur M. Hopkin Award, Chase Norman (2022) selected for Heidelberg Laureate Forum, Anjali Kantharuban (2022) Gates Cambridge Scholarship, Jennifer Grannen (2021) Timothy B. Campbell Innovation Award, Simon Li (2021) Arthur M. Hopkin Award, Leyla Kabuli (2021) Arthur M. Hopkin Award and University Medal.
chunks.question: What types of awards have Honors students received?
chunks.answer: Hopkin Award, Goldwater Scholarship, and Gates Cambridge.
